stories.headline: Oprah Winfrey admits she begged talk show audience to bury footage of Whitney Houston onstage fall - Fox News
stories.summary: Oprah Winfrey reveals Whitney Houston fell off the stage during a 2009 taping after relapsing on drugs, and she begged the audience not to share footage.

stories.headline: Bailey Zimmerman issues apology after facing charges for trashing local resort - KOB.com
stories.summary: Singer Bailey Zimmerman issued an apology statement on Tuesday, after charges were filed for an incident said to have caused $16,000 in damage at Sandia Resort & Casino.

stories.headline: Jack White’s Wife Olivia Jean Files for Divorce Over "Inappropriate Marital Conduct" - E! News
stories.summary: The White Stripes frontman Jack White's wife of four years Olivia Jean filed for divorce in Nashville, citing "irreconcilable difference and inappropriate marital conduct" as the reason for breakup.

stories.headline: ‘Ponies’ Cast React To Show’s Cancellation: Haley Lu Richardson, Vic Michaelis & More - Deadline
stories.summary: Ponies has been canceled after one season on Peacock, and the stars of the series are weighing in on social media.

stories.headline: Melissa Gilbert Hints at Husband Timothy Busfield’s Legal Woes Amid Moving Out of NYC Apartment - Yahoo
stories.summary: Melissa Gilbert is opening up about a major life change amid husband Timothy Busfield‘s criminal case. The Little House on the Prairie star, 62, announced in an Instagram post on Tuesday, June 23, th…

stories.headline: Disneyland guest climbs out of moving ride, slides down 52-foot drop on Tiana's Bayou Adventure - Entertainment Weekly
stories.summary: A 13-year-old Disneyland guest exited Tiana’s Bayou Adventure just before a sharp drop in the flume ride, sending him sliding approximately five watery stories to the bottom.

stories.headline: New Batman Movie Officially Confirms Bane as Main Villain in Epic Trailer - Collider
stories.summary: Batman officially returns to the world of movies in a stunning new trailer, reintroducing audiences to the fearsome Bane.
